target.name: Etched
target.founder: Gavin Uberti, Chris Zhu, Robert Wachen

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $5.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $2.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Etched is a Series A-stage semiconductor company that has raised $125M+ against a current post-money valuation of $5.0B. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 3 public semiconductor players (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private frontier-chip firms, with direct peer benchmarking against 3 same-stage companies: d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, applying a standard 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to the next funding round, implies a forward valuation of $10.0B on the $5.0B base. Peer-median anchoring across the three same-sector, same-stage comparables yields $1.0B, with a range of $500M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Etched's revenue is not on file; the broader sector trades at a 20.6x median P/S, but lacks the target metric for application.

The triangulated range is $850M (low) to $11.5B (high), with a median of $5.5B. The $5.0B current valuation sits near the center of this range, though reflects significant dispersion. Key uncertainties include the wide gap between private peer valuations ($500M–$2.0B) and the stage-progression result ($10.0B), and the absence of revenue data precluding revenue-multiple validation. The analysis is constrained by limited public precedent in this subsector and early-stage disclosure limitations.
